name: Status Panel
description: The panel now displays a detailed log overlay from the data chip. Among the rows of green text, one entry in BAY C-4 stands out in bright amber: OCCUPANT REVIVED - 242 YEARS AGO - STATUS: DEPARTED.
secret: Tapping a specific sequence on the panel pages through to other bays’ status, revealing one bay with an anomalous reading that suggests its occupant revived long ago.

scenery.description: The status 'DEPARTED' is rendered in a blocky, high-contrast font that pulses slowly on the panel. Unlike the standard logs, this entry lacks a subsequent 'RE-ENTRY' or 'DECEASED' flag, suggesting the individual simply walked out and never looked back.
scenery.rejection: The text is just a digital record; you cannot follow someone who left two centuries ago.

properties.takeRefusal: The panel is bolted to the wall.

name: Corridor C
description: A broad corridor with gently curved walls of brushed metal. Colored guide strips run along the floor — green toward the bow, red toward the stern. Recessed lighting panels overhead flicker intermittently, some dead entirely. A faded mural on one wall depicts people planting trees under a stylized sun. Below it, someone has scratched smaller figures in a different style.
secret: The scratched figures below the mural are arranged in a specific pattern — they are actually a map of the deck drawn by a later generation of inhabitants.

scenery.description: These primitive, thin-limbed stick figures are etched deeply into the metal, forming a sprawling web of intersecting lines and dots. They don't seem to be dancing; instead, each figure stands at a specific junction, their outstretched arms pointing toward peculiar geometric shapes.
scenery.rejection: The etchings are cold and permanent, scarred into the wall by a very sharp tool.

ai.prompt: The first step into the wider ship. The player should start noticing that this place has layers — the mural and the scratched figures hint that different people lived here at different times. This corridor connects the safe starting area to the rest of the deck. Objects here should be transitional: signage, wall markings, small hints of the ship's history.

name: The Commons - High Market
description: This vast, vaulted space was once the ship's social heartbeat, now muffled under heavy dust cloths and long shadows. Multi-level stalls and modular homes are built directly into the ship's hull, their facades decorated with fading murals and hanging bead curtains. A central fountain, now dry, is filled with a soft, bioluminescent moss that provides the only light in the gloom.
secret: The moss in the fountain is sensitive to sound. If the player 'sings' or makes noise, the moss pulses with light, briefly illuminating a hidden map carved into the ceiling of the plaza.

ai.imagePrompt: A massive, dark vaulted plaza inside a ship, illuminated by patches of glowing blue moss in a dry fountain. Ghostly shapes of furniture covered in grey dust cloths sit between colorful, tiered market stalls with hand-painted signs. The scale is immense, like a town square moved indoors, with balconies overlooking the space.

properties.takeRefusal: The space is too vast to move.

name: Junction C
description: Corridors branch in several directions from this hexagonal junction. A large directory console is mounted on the central pillar, its screen cracked but still displaying a partial deck map. The ceiling is higher here, and the air moves — somewhere a ventilation system still works. A bench built into the wall has been repurposed as a small shrine, covered in faded cloth and tiny carved figures.
secret: The shrine figures can be rearranged, and certain arrangements cause the directory console to display hidden deck sections that are otherwise marked RESTRICTED.

ai.prompt: This is the hub where the world opens up. The player should feel like there are many directions to go, each promising something different. The shrine is the first real cultural artifact — evidence that people here built meaning and ritual into their daily spaces. New content here should reinforce the sense of choice and branching possibility.

name: Archway to Junction C-7
description: A wide passage leading toward the bow. The guide strips here split into multiple colors. A sign reads THE COMMONS but the lights beyond are dark.

name: The Copper Arch
description: The passage back to the junction is marked by a tall, elegant archway of hammered copper.

name: The Low Tier Stepped Way
description: Broad, shallow stairs descend toward a lower level where the air feels cooler and smells of damp earth.

exit.destinationIntent: A lower-level marketplace or garden area of the Commons, perhaps more industrial or agricultural.

name: Merchant's Push-Cart
description: A wooden cart with wheels made from recycled gear-teeth, draped in a heavy, grey canvas. Through the gaps in the cloth, you can see rows of small glass jars and what looks like dried botanical specimens.

properties.takeRefusal: It's far too heavy to push through the dust.

name: Fountain Inscription
description: Around the rim of the dry fountain, letters have been painstakingly hammered into the metal. The moss glows brighter when you lean close.

properties.takeRefusal: The inscription is part of the fountain's heavy rim.

name: Gurgling Bubble-Thrower
description: This brass-fitted apparatus looks like a trumpet crossed with a chemistry set, featuring a hand-crank and a reservoir of shimmering, viscous fluid. A small sign on the side reads: 'COMMUNAL JOY DISPENSER - PROPERTY OF THE AERATED COALITION.'
secret: If the crank is turned while the device is submerged in the mossy fountain, it produces 'heavy' bubbles that sink and roll along the floor, potentially triggering floor-based pressure plates or revealing hidden drafts.

properties.takeRefusal: The device is quite heavy, but the leather strap makes it portable.

authoring.creationCommand: ai create a gun that is silly

name: Ornery Service-Bot
description: A squat, rectangular maintenance droid with a single glowing eye-sensor and two articulating claw-arms. It is currently blocking the path northwest, its chassis vibrating with a low, menacing hum. A speaker grill on its front emits a series of aggressive squawks and digital hisses whenever you approach.

properties.takeRefusal: The bot clamps its treads to the floor and makes a very alarming 'REDACTED' sound.

name: Vial of Joy Concentrate
description: A small glass vial filled with a thick, iridescent blue liquid. The label features a logo of a smiling person surrounded by bubbles and the words: 'JOY CONCENTRATE - REFILL FOR ALL AERATED COALITION DISPENSERS.'

properties.shortDescription: a vial of iridescent blue fluid

name: The Weaving District
description: A narrower street leads toward a section draped in thousands of swaying, multi-colored strands.

name: The Weaving District
description: A labyrinth of suspended walkways, bridges of braided cable, and hanging dwellings. Everywhere you look, long strands of colored fiber sway in the artificial breeze, some being worked by clattering automated looms. The air is thick with the scent of dyed wool and machine oil.

name: Market Archway
description: The way back to the main market area.
